title: Providing for congressional disapproval under chapter 8 of title 5, United States Code, of the rule submitted by the Environmental Protection Agency relating to "Reconsideration of the Dust-Lead Hazard Standards and Dust-Lead Post-Abatement Clearance Levels".
plain_summary: This bill lets Congress reject a new rule made by the Environmental Protection Agency about lead dust safety standards. The EPA rule was about how much lead dust is considered dangerous and what levels are safe after cleaning up lead. If Congress passes this bill, the EPA rule would be canceled and would not become law. This means the old standards for lead dust safety would stay in place instead of the new ones the EPA wanted to make.
last_action_text: Referred to the House Committee on Energy and Commerce.

sections.title: Joint Resolution
sections.text: Congress has decided to reject a rule made by the Environmental Protection Agency. This rule was about lead dust safety standards and cleanup requirements. The rule was called Reconsideration of the Dust-Lead Hazard Standards and Dust-Lead Post-Abatement Clearance Levels. It was published in the Federal Register on November twelfth, two thousand twenty-four. Because Congress disapproves of this rule, it will have no power and cannot be enforced.
